title: Volutarella erucifolia

Further research is needed to confirm the exact classification of Volutarella erucifolia.

intro: Volutarella erucifolia, also known as Wild Rocket or Hoary Cress, is a biennial plant in the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its distinctive, edible leaves and flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects, making it beneficial for treating various health issues such as digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Volutarella erucifolia include glucosinolates, isothiocyanates, and flavonoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments and promote overall well-being.
